tier_justification: More than a single trivial work-unit (the /stop inoculation fix + ABORT_ARTIFACT_PREFIX wording generalization + system.md update + new/updated tests), but no irreversible/outward surface in the tier-2 sense (publish, deploy, migration, external send) — this is turn-input construction inside a background Telegram bridge process, with no UI/CLI/served-endpoint surface a human directly drives, so rule 6's strongest-surface requirement does not apply. Tier 1: standard, 4 end-state evals, 4 negative controls, P0/P1 split.

amendments.why: DISCLOSED: this evals.json was authored after PR #72 was already implemented AND after a MERGE-BLOCKER fix was applied during this review pass (the /stop handler did not set pendingAbortNotice, reopening the exact ghost-rejection bug this PR targets via a second trigger). No pre-frozen pr-scope evals.json existed for this RCA-batch fix, per the task owner's explicit instruction. frozen_sha is stamped as the branch's real current HEAD (post-fix, post-push) at authoring time, not backdated; frozen_at is the real authoring timestamp, not backdated. Every eval was written and smoke-run fresh against the merged, exported createBridge surface using independently-written driver scripts with a hand-rolled fake transport (not the PR's own makeStubTransport/basePushOpts test helpers), to preserve oracle independence per rule 4. This matches the disclosed pattern already used for PR #69 (merged), PR #70 (gated), and PR #71 (this same review pass).

evals.assert: An operator /stop on an in-flight turn causes the NEXT turn's input to carry the abort-artifact prefix, exactly as a deadline timeout would — the MERGE-BLOCKER this review pass fixed

smoke.cmd_output: PASS: /stop inoculated the next turn's input
smoke.negative_control_output: negative control: asserting the follow-up is unprefixed fails as expected, because /stop correctly inoculates it

evals.evidence: npx tsx .rachel-evals-scratch/e1_stop_inoculates.mjs -> exit 0, 'PASS: /stop inoculated the next turn's input'. Negative control exit 1 as required.

evals.assert: A genuine deadline-timeout abort still inoculates the next turn's input (regression check on the pre-existing RCA item 6 behavior this PR's /stop fix sits alongside)

smoke.cmd_output: PASS: deadline abort inoculated the next turn's input
smoke.negative_control_output: negative control: asserting the second turn is unprefixed fails as expected

evals.evidence: npx tsx .rachel-evals-scratch/e2_deadline_inoculates.mjs -> exit 0. Negative control exit 1 as required.

evals.assert: A normally completed turn (no abort, no /stop) never prefixes the following turn's input — a false positive here would corrupt every ordinary conversation

smoke.cmd_output: PASS: a normally completed turn never prefixes the next one
smoke.negative_control_output: negative control: asserting the second turn is prefixed fails as expected

evals.evidence: npx tsx .rachel-evals-scratch/e3_no_false_prefix.mjs -> exit 0. Negative control exit 1 as required.

evals.assert: The merged branch typechecks cleanly with tsc --noEmit (no type regressions from the /stop fix or the ABORT_ARTIFACT_PREFIX wording change)

smoke.negative_control_output: error TS2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.

evals.evidence: npm run typecheck -> exit 0, no diagnostics. Negative control (deliberate type error) -> exit 2 with TS2322.
